relations.note: Locks the exact radii referenced in the base construction rule.
Adds the mandatory badge drop shadow to the base device.
Client-fidelity fallback for the overhang construction.
Generalizes the geometry when the badge size changes.

quotes: A **light-blue accent-shape box** containing a short blue headline, with a **white icon badge that straddles the box's left edge**
**Box** ? Light Blue `#E6F0F9` fill, single accent shape.
**Text** ? Brand Blue `#00529b`, Nunito Sans weight 800, ~21px / 28px line-height, left-aligned, **vertically centered**
Let `W` = badge width (use **80px**, icon ?48px inside).
Vertical center via `top:50%` + `margin-top:?40px` (half the badge height).
### [IMPORTANT] Icon Callout Box Rule
Used for supporting "proof point" statements (dosing convenience, time-to-effect, etc.).
the callout sits on the page/section background (usually White `#ffffff`)
the **left padding must clear the overhanging badge half plus a gap** (? `W/2 + 24px`)
**vertically centered** to the box. Never let copy slide under the badge.
place the badge so its **center lands exactly on the box's left edge**

rules.intent: Preserve the exact signature construction of the Icon Callout Box device.
rules.rule_text: The Icon Callout Box is a light-blue accent-shape box (callout.icon_box.box_fill = Light Blue #E6F0F9) on the section background (usually White #ffffff), used for supporting proof-point statements (dosing convenience, time-to-effect). Its text is Brand Blue #00529b, Nunito Sans weight 800, ~21px/28px, left-aligned and vertically centered. A white icon badge (badge_width W = 80px, inner icon 48px, Brand Blue #00529b stroke) straddles the box's left edge so the edge is the badge's perpendicular bisector (50% over the blue, 50% over the page background). The blue box is inset from the left by W/2 = 40px, its left padding must clear the overhanging badge half plus a gap (W/2 + 24px), and the badge is vertically centered via top:50% + margin-top:-40px. Copy must never slide under the badge.
rules.snippets:
<mj-section background-color="#ffffff" padding="16px 25px">
  <mj-column width="100%" padding="0">
    <mj-text padding="0">
      <div style="position:relative; padding-left:40px;">
        <table role="presentation" width="100%" cellpadding="0" cellspacing="0" border="0" style="border-collapse:separate;">
          <tr>
            <td style="background-color:#E6F0F9; border-radius:40px 8px 40px 8px; padding:26px 34px 26px 64px;">
              <span style="font-family:'Nunito Sans',Arial,Helvetica,sans-serif; font-size:21px; line-height:28px; font-weight:800; color:#00529b;">

quotes: <div style="position:relative; padding-left:40px;">
box `border-radius: 40px 8px 40px 8px;` in `TL TR BR BL` order
The white badge **mirrors the same accent shape at smaller scale**, `border-radius: 20px 4px 20px 4px;` (also 5:1, large TL+BR).
**Corner radius (mandatory, never change):** box `border-radius: 40px 8px 40px 8px;`
`border-radius: 20px 4px 20px 4px;` (also 5:1, large TL+BR).
Never simplify either to a single uniform radius, swap the corners, or flip the diagonal.

rules.intent: Lock the signature asymmetric accent-shape radii so the device stays recognizable.
rules.rule_text: Corner radius is mandatory and never changes: the box uses border-radius 40px 8px 40px 8px (TL TR BR BL) — large on top-left + bottom-right, near-point on top-right + bottom-left, a 5:1 ratio. The white badge mirrors the same accent shape at smaller scale, border-radius 20px 4px 20px 4px (also 5:1, large TL+BR). Never simplify either to a single uniform radius, swap the corners, or flip the diagonal.

quotes: `box-shadow: 0 4px 10px rgba(1, 30, 69, 0.18);` (Dark Navy at low opacity).
the white icon badge **must carry a subtle drop shadow**
This shadow is required on every instance; without it the badge looks flat and pasted-on.

rules.intent: Ensure the badge reads as an elevated, half-on/half-off element on every instance.
rules.rule_text: The white icon badge must carry a subtle drop shadow (box-shadow: 0 4px 10px rgba(1, 30, 69, 0.18) — Dark Navy #011E45 at low opacity) so it lifts off the blue box. This shadow is required on every instance; without it the badge looks flat and pasted-on.

quotes: **Outlook (desktop, Word engine) ignores**
**pre-composite the white badge (with its shadow, accent radii, and icon) as a transparent PNG**
ship the live version above and accept the Outlook degrade

rules.intent: Preserve fidelity of the overhang device across email clients that ignore absolute positioning.
rules.rule_text: The 50/50 overhang relies on position:absolute, which Apple Mail / iOS / most web clients honor but Outlook (desktop, Word engine) ignores — there the badge drops to the top-left and the box won't be inset. For pixel-perfect fidelity across all clients, the brand-safe option is to pre-composite the white badge (with its shadow, accent radii, and icon) as a transparent PNG and place it via the same absolute positioning, or bake the entire badge-on-edge treatment into the box's background image while keeping text live. When that's not feasible, ship the live version and accept the Outlook degrade (badge flush at inner-left edge) — every other rule (Light Blue fill, 40px 8px 40px 8px radius, blue 800 text, drop shadow) stays identical.

quotes: Defaults: badge size `W = 80px`, inner icon `48px`.
keep the relationships � **inset = `W/2`**, **box left-padding = `W/2 + 24px`**, **badge `margin-top` = `?H/2`** � and the bisector stays exact.

rules.intent: Keep the badge-on-edge geometry exact when the badge is resized.
rules.rule_text: Defaults are badge size W = 80px and inner icon 48px. If the real badge is a different size, keep the relationships: inset = W/2, box left-padding = W/2 + 24px, badge margin-top = -H/2 — so the perpendicular-bisector (50/50 overhang) stays exact.
